item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was an extremely fun read, and a fresh concept that I haven't seen before.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personal take: This is a war between two different anomalies, and both are trying to rig history to support their stance better. Neither is actually being completely honest. The birds are trying to forge the extent of their claim to dinohood, the ghost dinosaurs are a reptile counterplay.

Neither accurately represents the dinos of an untampered past. (To me, this makes it more absurd, not less.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem I have with this skip is that if the Special Containment Procedures are being implemented in "our world", they're not working very well. Like there's tons of accessible research and information about various dinosaurs with feathers. I just expect Special Containment Procedures to kind of fit into how the world works. Just a personal preference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not saying I hate this skip - I love skips that take absurd ideas and melodramatically play them straight. This skip is so utterly wacky it's impossible not to snigger at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing is that the birds do think the Earth rightfully belongs to them. If birds didn't evolve from dinosaurs, and are trying to fraud themselves into Earth's fossil record, where the heck did they come from? Not Earth, but they don't want you to know that.

And you don't have to change anything. "There is no canon" means that you can put a feathered raptor on the site and not have to worry that it contradicts this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a love-hate relationship with this skip. When I first read it, I thought that birds were starting to atavistically remembering their glorious past as dinosaurs, and it causes a mass hysteria in which birds throw themselves onto fossils to show that the Earth rightfully belongs to them.

I loved that concept, and ended up being very disappointed when the twist made it into a "feathers ruined dinosaurs" skip rather than a celebration of feathered dinosaurs and the real-world strangeness of bird evolution.

(I'm also a bit worried that I'll have to alter a couple of my SCP ideas, which feature feathered dinosaurs; because I think scaly raptors are overrated).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually on my way through again working up from 4000.
One of the best so far. +1 again if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's actually a good point re: the eventual digging deeper, perhaps I should add a line to the DYNASTY protocol about creating a Foundation-approved replacement story.

I rationalized it as the Foundation judging the Ghost Dinosaurs as harder to physically deal with, and doing more visible damage than the birds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: really interesting, I like the bit about the zombie dinosaurs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is completely absurd in a really lovely way.
The only thing bothering me are the containment procedures: disproving publicly the current evolutionary theory would inevitably lead to worldwide research into the actual, apparently anomalous, origins of birds. Wouldn't it be so much more convenient for the Foundation to go along with the birds' plan?

(anyway, +1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of the reason this skip's premise (birds framed dinosaurs as their ancestors) works so well for me is because it plays into the trope of the devil planting fossils to fool us — a premise so ridiculous at its core that it's more fun for me to see others playing with it than mocking or deriding it.

Admittedly, I didn't grow up in a home that taught me evolution was a lie — so I understand how people who did might not appreciate a narrative that, in a silly way, reaffirms that narrative. But for me, there's a lot more fun to be had in exploring why the moon landing was faked rather than exploring why it so clearly wasn't.

Not that anyone's wrong for feeling otherwise, of course! I just wanted to offer my two cents as to why a skip about birds faking evolution absolutely works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, birds don't need dinosaurs. Birds are already basically anime characters.

Loved the skip. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved the first few paragraphs. My initial impression was that the birds are atavistically remembering their glorious evolutionary past as dinosaurs, and it causes a mass hysteria in which birds throw themselves onto fossils as a sacrifice to revive their ancestors.

I loved that concept, and ended up being very disappointed when the twist made it into a joking dig at the expense of feathered dinosaurs, instead of a celebration of feathered dinosaurs and the real-world strangeness of bird evolution. imo, big scaly lizard dinosaurs actually feel a lot more mundane than the feathered kind, in part because that image is overrepresented in ficiton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. However…

Dinosaurs with feathers fucking rock, okay? Just letting you know. They look rad as hell.

item.title: Re: Pretty good article.
item.content:encoded:
That's…that's kinda the point. You're missing what the article is trying to say.

Besides, there aren't really rules saying that the universe portrayed in an SCP must exactly mimics real life. Maybe in our world dinosaurs have feathers, but not in this article specifically. That's like, the definition of a fiction piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In other words, I second DeadlyMoose's post.

I am also obsessed with prehistoric life.

item.title: Pretty good article.
item.content:encoded:
[see above]

Still, -1 because DINOSAURS HAD FEATHERS. Y'all gotta acknowledge it.

I read it for the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Totally understandable :)

And ah, thanks for the clarification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, it's not your fault. We have different tastes and that's totally fine. I just find the real things more appealing is all, I guess.
(Also, for more information on the term "awesomebro", I suggest looking at this video. https://youtu.be/2NJJTAse1Eg)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesomebro SCP

I'm confused, is this just a compliment or a reference to something?

In any case, sorry the bird conspiracy wasn't enough to counteract the whole featherless part for you ¯\_(' > ')_/¯

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is literally just an Awesomebro SCP.
I really want to like this, but I can't because I absolutely despise the whole "science ruined dinosaurs" trope with every fiber of my being, and it just overall left a really bad taste in my mouth. Don't get me wrong, the idea of the controversy being started by birds of all things is funny as hell, but at the end of the day this SCP just feels like its trying to pander to people who are blinded by nostalgia.
No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically the Macedonia controversy with birds instead of FYROM and dinosaurs instead of Greece.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The implication was supposed to be that the birds left the raptor head in the trunk as a gangster-esque "send em a message", not that the head itself caused the "accident". Originally, I explicitly had the birds just swarm her windshield and cause her to swerve into the truck, but I was advised at the time to change it. Do you think reverting that would help?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was on board right up until the last line. I was expecting excessive volumes of bird poop on the windshield, or a telltale feather stuck in the grill, or at least bite marks all over the driver's body. But instead, we're to believe they caused the accident by suddenly making the trunk heavier? That got me laughing out loud and completely killed my suspension of disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(DYNASTY was originally the HAM protocol in the draft, as a nod to Ken Ham

hahaha oh man I bet we have the exact same books. I still have 'em all, tbh. (A lot of the creationist dinosaur book art circa the 90's was legitimately great. this one was particularly stunning just for the artistic flai, and I was pretty obssesed with this one. Or this one and this one for non-creationist ones.)

Paleoart with feathered dinosaurs started out really bad, but it's gotten overall excellent by now. That's what converted me to loving the updated versions, but it won't help with most peoples' nostalgia, of course!

I think honestly this is mostly a personal reaction from me. I doubt it's a really big problem overall for the SCP, especially since it's basically a joke anyway, and everyone's gonna see it that way. Very few people come from my POV, at present.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was raised creationist (DYNASTY was originally the HAM protocol in the draft, as a nod to Ken Ham), so I can kinda see where you're coming from. That said, I also have a huge nostalgia for Jurassic Park and my childhood dinosaur books, and gosh darnit, I just prefer the featherless versions on a purely aesthetic ground, so the article is a teensy little bit wish-fulfillment on that part.

There's also a piece of me that is aggravated by pedants or smartasses going on about how "technically, there's a dinosaur in your birdcage/bombing your windshield/on the Thanksgiving table", which punches my inner child in the face the same way telling em Santa isn't real does. (Part of the inspiration was that if this annoyed me, imagine how the dinos feel!)

Early on I figured making the birds responsible would help make the big "everything is a lie" a bit easier to swallow, by framing it in the context of a larger, wackier phenomenon, but it's OK if that wasn't enough in your case.

Glad you still found it enjoyable, in any case :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The trope of "nostalgia is right, science is wrong" bothers me pretty deeply. Especially in this case, since I grew up in (what was effectively) a fundamentalist cult and was obsessed with dinosaurs, so I read aaaaall about how the feather stuff was a giant horrible conspiracy.

Making it a conspiracy by birds is much funnier, but still breaks my suspension of disbelief. Especially due to the tiresome recent goings-on about how Jurassic Park style zombie dinosaurs are Objectively Better and feathers Ruin Dinosaurs Forever. (I do love Jurassic Park dinosaurs, but as a paleoart enthusiast, I'm honestly more frustrated with the rhetoric than I should be.)

This is really more of a personal critique, though, and obviously it's not fair to the article. None of this existing context is the article's fault!

I do think the article is enjoyable, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Team Bird returns, I see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's with this site and nefarious birds? Anyway the concept is pretty novel, I laughs hard when I figured out what's going on. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Appreciate the good review, Conwell, and I'm glad you enjoyed.

birds performing these time travel rituals to … forge a fraudulent claim to the dinosaur throne

It's sentences like this, that can only happen here, that remind me why I initially fell in love with this site and why I keep writing for it.

I had figured most of the entries would be super dead serious (now that the pressure to finish my own is gone, I'll soon go back and read them all and see for myself), I wanted something a little more fun for my own contribution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While this is not the first Ghost Dinosaur article on the site, it is certainly a welcome addition.

The combination of birds performing these time travel rituals to plant evidence to essentially forge a fraudulent claim to the dinosaur throne is quite creative, and the ghost dinosaurs fighting back adds for an interesting, if brief, incident log. Additionally, Barrera essentially getting assassinated by the birds, in the end, is a particularly solid ending, showing the lengths the birds are willing to go to.

+1. Fun article, weryl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending sold it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually agree that I think this is a better regular article than a 4k, but, whoops, reflexively upvoted, oh well.

Ghost dinosaurs vs. history-rewriting birds, and all over feathers. :D I'm so into this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it really is minor, but eh, decided to throw it in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) I'm not gonna reveal, as there are at least two possible answers here (that I had in mind; more are certainly possible), one of which might be fridge logic, and I felt that leaving it up to interpretation was more fun. I may assess audience response and either give hints here or by making some edits.

2) dramatic effect, mostly. She's a paranoid conspiracy theorist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
all my life i've been utterly goddamn certain that birds are up to something. just look at them. look at their horrible little soulless eyes. look at how they move.

this has all the characteristic elegance, directness, and efficiency that i've come to expect from a Weryllium article. and it's got that magic mix of unique premise, slick presentation, and lingering questions for the reader to chew on afterward, without any hand-holding or sign-waving. a quality product, my good chum ♪ +1

AND HA HA I WAS FUCKING RIGHT. I'M WISE TO YOUR GAME, BIRDS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really minor, YMMV kind of thing. In my experience, me and a good 80% of people I interact with are Hispanic and use varying ratios of Spanglish, ranging all the way from 99% Spanish with occasional English phrases peppered in, to the opposite. Especially with people they're familiar with, as the two of them are here. If it turns out that I'm the weird one here I can easily change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well Alfred Hitchcock would upvote. MTF Phi-2 “Clever Girls” was a good smile. I guess my lingering questions are (1) what is the birds’ motive?…phylogenetic clout? and (2) why is she whispering the reveal at the end?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a 4K contest entry: I honestly don't like this at all.

but, as just another, good ol' article, I like this. It does what it needs to, and while I don't 100% get why the birds do this, it's an interesting article.

just one nitpick
Sudden Gratuitous Spanish

The only nitpick I have is the sudden "si" in there.

In personal experience, people tend to speak the same language continuously unless they don't know or forget a certain word they want to use, in which case, they would resort to say that word in another language they're more fluent in1, but in this case, the person already demonstrated they know how to say "yes" and "yeah" (also it's a really basic word), so it kinda doesn't make sense.

note that YMMV bout this, this might just be me

Footnotes
1. For example: "[…] ..Yeah, the restaurant was great.. but my problem with the place's-.. fuck, how's it called.. comida? was that it had.. […]"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is very difficult for me to hide my excitement for another dinosaur entry in the 4k contest.

🦕💖🦖

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, sorry, but nothing here has really changed since I critted this. It's still bare-bones in my opinion, and I just cannot enjoy this. Sorry, but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"History" is about the worst possible prompt I could have received, after "politics". Thankfully I had this idea lying around since shortly after JamCon, and finally figured out how to expand it into a complete skip.

HUGE thanks to The Great Hippo for all his help crystallizing the concept, as well as polishing the final product. Thanks to Modern_Erasmus for inspiring this concept initially with his excellent SCP-3940, and thanks to the people who read the draft: 9Volt, Uncle Nicolini, Captain Kirby, stormbreath, and Sir Baubius.

Image from https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Starling_Roost_(6781835682).jpg and is CC 2.0.
